meta.name: Thai Red Curry (Gaeng Phed)
meta.subtitle: Rich coconut red curry with red curry paste, tender protein, and vegetables—balanced salty-sweet with a bright finish.

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Deterministic canonical red curry recipe for SSA catalogs.
Designed for store-bought red curry paste with optional fresh boosts.
Chicken default with tofu/seafood/vegetarian adaptations included.

summary: A classic Thai red curry built by frying red curry paste in coconut cream, simmering with chicken and vegetables, then balancing with fish sauce and palm sugar for a creamy, aromatic bowl best served with jasmine rice.

yield.notes: Serves 4 with rice; 3 large bowls without sides.

notes: Freezing is acceptable; coconut milk may separate—reheat gently and whisk.
Red curry deepens overnight; heat can feel stronger the next day.

tasteProfile.notes: Red curry is typically warmer and rounder than green, with dried chili depth. Paste brands vary widely.

required.label: Medium pot or Dutch oven
required.notes: A heavy pot helps prevent scorching coconut cream.

ingredients.label: Coconut milk (full-fat), 2 cans
notes: Use the thick cream to fry the paste first; then add remaining liquid.

ingredients.label: Red curry paste
notes: Start with ~2–3 Tbsp depending on brand. Increase later if desired.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock (or water)
ingredients.notes: Use water if needed; stock adds depth.

ingredients.label: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Primary salting agent for Thai curries.

ingredients.label: Zucchini, half-moons (or Thai eggplant if available)
Eggplant, bite-size (optional veg)
ingredients.notes: Use Japanese/Chinese eggplant if Thai eggplant is unavailable.

ingredients.label: Lime juice (to finish)
ingredients.notes: Brightens the curry; add after turning off heat.

steps.title: Prep protein and vegetables
instructions: Slice vegetables: bell pepper strips, zucchini half-moons, trim green beans; drain bamboo shoots.
Pick Thai basil leaves; keep ready for the end.
checks: Everything is prepped before heating coconut cream (it can scorch if unattended).

steps.title: Fry red curry paste in coconut cream
instructions: Spoon the thick coconut cream portion into a pot over medium-high heat.
Add red curry paste and stir constantly until fragrant and slightly darkened, about 2–3 minutes.
If coconut milk is thin, use the whole can and cook a little longer, stirring to avoid burning.
checks: Paste smells fragrant (not raw) and nothing is sticking/burning.

steps.title: Build the curry base
instructions: Whisk in remaining coconut milk and chicken stock (or water).
checks: Curry is simmering gently, not boiling hard.

steps.title: Simmer chicken until just cooked
instructions: Add chicken to the simmering curry.
Simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through.
checks: Chicken is opaque throughout and tender.

steps.title: Add vegetables and simmer until tender-crisp
instructions: Add vegetables (choose what you're using): zucchini/eggplant first, then beans, then bell pepper and bamboo shoots.
Simmer gently until tender but not mushy.
checks: Vegetables are cooked but still have shape and color.

steps.title: Season, balance, and finish with basil
instructions: Stir in fish sauce and palm sugar; simmer 1 minute to dissolve.
Taste and adjust: more fish sauce for salt/umami; more sugar to round heat; more coconut milk to mellow spice.
Turn off heat. Fold in Thai basil and add lime juice if using.
checks: Flavor is balanced: creamy, savory-sweet with clean heat.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Optional: offer lime wedges and chili flakes at the table.

pairings.notes: Plain rice is the classic base; crisp, acidic sides help cut coconut richness.

leftovers.notes: Reheat gently; whisk if coconut separates. Add fresh basil if possible.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly and store airtight. Reheat gently to prevent coconut breaking.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight and whisk while reheating.

notes: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

adaptations.title: Curry paste brand variability
notes: Heat/salt varies a lot; start small and add more after a short simmer.
If curry tastes harsh, simmer a few extra minutes and add coconut milk.
If curry is flat, adjust fish sauce (umami/salt) and sugar (roundness).

ingredient.chicken_thigh.notes: Use 400–500g tofu. Pan-sear cubes first for best texture, then add near the end.

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Season to taste; keep salty-sweet balance.

ingredient.shrimp.label: Shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredient.shrimp.notes: Add in the last 3–4 minutes; cook just until opaque.

notes: Cook shrimp at the end to prevent toughness.

ingredient.red_curry_paste.notes: Start with less paste; add more only if desired.

notes: Add extra coconut milk if you want it even milder.
Key technique: fry curry paste in coconut cream to bloom aromatics and deepen flavor.
Add Thai basil off-heat to keep it green and fragrant.
Vegetables are flexible—keep a mix of tender and crisp textures.

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount value/unit (or optional flagged).
All steps include id+title and instructions[] with timers where useful.
